observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a) The Inventory Committee to prioritize the taking of physical count of PPEs
for a specified period, to facilitate the preparation of the required RPCPPE; and

b) The Department Heads to cooperate with the Inventory Committee in
facilitating the preparation of the RPCPPE.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Agriculturist to:

a) Facilitate the proper turn-over of the facility from the private operator to the
LGU and coordinate immediately with the PhilMech for the proper
execution and compliance with the undertakings set by the MOA; and

b) Request from PhilMech a copy of the signed AR and PAR to determine
proprietary rights of the LGU over the facility.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a) Direct the Inventory Committee, particularly the concerned department or
office heads, to dutifully comply with E.O. No. 21 dated August 2, 2023 and
establish effective policies on the timely submission of complete
documentation and preparation of RPCI and RPCSP as mandated by
Section 124 of the Manual and COA Circular 2022-004; and

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances in the
Subsidiary Ledger and General Ledger and the prior years’ balances.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements of the composition of the Cash-Local Treasury account
as well as the subsequent events affecting the CLT account such as the date
of the eventual deposit of the reported undeposited collections as at year-end
to provide complete and reliable financial information to users of the
financial statements;

b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate
and come up with additional measures, such as continuing the use of google
sheets in sharing reports on collections and deposits to help in the
identification of variances in the accounting and treasury records and thus
aid in the reconciliation process; and

c) Upon reconciliation, direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to adjust accordingly in their respective records, the identified
valid causes of variances such as errors and/or omissions to reflect the
correct amount of Cash-Local Treasury account in the financial statements
and prevent further variances.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Treasurer to continue its practice of adhering with the receipt and collection
process, specifically on the maintenance of cashbook as provided in the NGAS
Manual for LGUs as well as other applicable laws and regulations to ensure proper
and adequate monitoring and supervision by the MT of the cash collection and
management of the LGU.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the status of the reconciling
items and make the necessary adjusting entries as well as ensure regular and
diligent reconciliation of records to prevent further unlocated differences and
variances; and

b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the bank in securing
monthly bank statement for the LCTD account which will aid the Municipal
Accountant’s preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statement.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
strictly require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to establish regular
conduct of reconciliation of their records to prevent further variances.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accounting Office to review the entries made to several
Advances to SDOs accounts to determine the cause of the negative balance and to
reconcile the variances noted between the subsidiary and general ledger, as well as
to diligently monitor the account to prevent these variances from further increasing.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a) Conduct in-depth evaluation to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the
recorded payables, particularly those which remain unsettled for more than
three to 20 years already to determine which obligations have valid
claimants;

b) Take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the
Municipality for those transactions proven to be without valid claimants
and/or dormant accounts for fair presentation of liabilities in the financial
statements; and

c) Continue the reconciliation of the balances of the accounts payable under the
General Fund-Proper and General Fund-EDF to identify the possible causes
of variances such as errors/omissions in recording and adjust them
accordingly in the records in order to reflect the accurate amount of
Accounts Payable in the financial statements as well as the Aging Schedule.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to:

a) Review the records pertaining to prior years to reconcile the concerned
accounts as well as the noted variances between the GL and SL balances; and

b) Ensure that remittances are made in full and intact for all taxes and
contributions withheld and prevent the variances between the GL and SL
balance from increasing.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a) Conduct reconciliation of the dormant and unreconciled accounts to
determine its actual status;

b) Conduct reconciliation of the SL and GL balances to determine the cause of
the noted variances and effect the necessary adjustments; and

c) Prepare the correcting entry for the financial assistance received amounting
to ₱100,000.00.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the withholding and remittance of the
employees’ loans under the Other Payables account. Likewise, to reconcile the
balances in the Subsidiary Ledger, General Ledger, and prior years’ balances to
correct the negative balance of the account in the Financial Statement.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Formulate and adopt measures to intensify revenue collection efforts by
strictly enforcing existing tax laws and ordinances within the Municipality; and

b) Ensure the proper conduct of periodic assessment/evaluation of collection
accomplishments in order to warrant the tapping of all sources to maximize
the LGU’s revenue-generating powers as mandated by law.

We recommended and the Management agreed that Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to locate the supporting
documents of prior years' transactions to be able to reconcile the GL and SL
balances and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prospectively furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list of the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year;
require the Municipal Accountant to record the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year to avoid understatement of receivable accounts and misrepresentation of the financial statements, as a whole; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation between general ledger and subsidiary ledger balances in order to arrive at an accurate balance of receivable accounts, and also to reconcile the subsidiary ledgers per year to avoid negative ending balances and facilitate matching of actual collections against the deferred revenues.

We recommended that Management:
require the coordination of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials;
require the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of inventories per type and prepare the RPCI within the period required by Section 124 of the Manual to properly account for the remaining inventory balance at year end; and
require the personnel or department heads to submit the necessary documents for the liquidation of inventories that were already distributed and/or consumed under their custody to serve as the basis for adjustment of the Accounting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
create an inventory committee composed of regular employees whose only duties or functions shall be to conduct on a full time basis, an actual physical count of PPEs for a specified period of time, to facilitate proper accounting and reporting of fixed properties; and
designate one personnel each from MTO and Accounting Office who shall conduct the reconciliation of respective records to present the accurate value of PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
review and ensure that the withheld amount of taxes due to BIR and contribution and loans due to GSIS are fully remitted;
review the subsidiary records pertaining to prior years to reconcile the variance on beginning balances; and
prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous entries made on Due to GSIS and furnish the Audit Team copies of such journal entries for purposes of validation.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
record an adjusting entry in the books of the subsequent fiscal year to recognize Due to Officers and Employees account and derecognize Accounts Payable for any benefits included in the ₱2.18 million which remained unpaid as of this writing; and
refrain from recording Accounts Payable for transactions that do not fall under its prescribed description to avoid overstatement of such account and understatement of another liability account where the transaction should have been recorded.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous entries made on “Due to GSIS” account as regards refund due to moratorium and furnish the Audit Team copies of such journal entries for purposes of validation;
review and ensure that the withheld amounts for salary loans are fully remitted; and
reconcile the subsidiary ledger with the general ledger records.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and other personnel to submit the required financial reports and lacking documents within the prescribed period; and
strictly require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the unsubmitted vouchers and journals together with the supporting documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit of paid transactions.

We recommended that the Management enforce the guidelines on liquidation of cash advance as the purpose for which the ₱10.90 million cash advanced for SAP distribution has already been achieved, by complying the necessary and complete liquidation documents supporting the distribution of SAP, in accordance with the MC mentioned above.

We recommended that the Management through the MSWDO and Officer in charge of the SAP  initiate efforts to address the noted variances, to wit: (1) the actual number of beneficiaries versus those encoded in the system,  to account for the amount of ₱0.77 million; (2) the profile of the 227 recipients  who received the P1.14 million of the SAP but were not included in the SACUP, to determine their eligibility; (3) the issue on 31 recipients corresponding to P155,000.00, to ensure that there was no duplication of recipients on the benefits granted.

We recommended that Management:
continue formulating responsive programs and projects to properly utilize the funds appropriated by the LGU and implement the same in accordance to the Annual and Supplemental Budgets approved by the Sangguniang Bayan; and
ensure that projects, programs, activities and expenses implemented by the LGU will not exceed the appropriated amounts as found in the Annual and Supplemental Budgets.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Accountant and other persons responsible in completing the supporting documents to submit the soonest time to the Auditor’s Office the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents and the corresponding journal entry vouchers; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that subsequent required reports and accounts with complete supporting documents are submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in her capacity as MSB Chairman:
require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and other concerned person(s) to comply with the indicative timelines provided in Joint Circular No. 2017-1 to aid the DepEd PSDS in her timely preparation of SEF Budget; and
direct the MSB Vice Chairman and other officials concerned to prepare the SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 presented by program, activity and project (PAP), by allotment class and per school as prescribed in the Joint Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
avoid the recognition of payables in the books prior to actual receipt of goods or services, and of expenses without actual issuance or consumption of supplies or materials; and
attach the necessary and complete documentary requirements to corroborate the validity and propriety of recorded Accounts Payable accounts.

We recommended that Management:
instruct the GFPS to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget on a timely basis, following the schedule and format prescribed by JMC 2016-01, and submit the same to the DILG Provincial Office for their review and endorsement;
ensure that the cost of implementation of activities that address identified gender issues and concerns for the year totalled at least five percent of the annual budget; and
direct the GFPS to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report on a timely basis, following the schedule and format prescribed by JMC 2016-01, and submit the same to the DILG Provincial Office for their review and consolidation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all Department Heads/employees concerned to submit detailed action plans on the CY 2019 AAR recommendations including that of prior years, on the areas related/connected to their department/offices and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI and submit the same to the COA audit team; and
We also recommended that henceforth, within sixty days from their receipt of the AAR, submit to the COA Auditor, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the action taken on the said audit findings and recommendations and strictly adhere to the provision of COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 with regard to the preparation of said document.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated Cashbook for Cash in
Bank-LCCA account which shall be readily available for inspection anytime
during office hours; and
b) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
respective records and observe the highest standards of prudence and
consistency in the keeping of accounts as safeguard against inaccurate or
misleading information to stakeholders.

We recommended that Management:
a) send demand letters to the concerned AOs to enforce the liquidation of
their cash advances;
b) require the Accountable Officers concerned to settle/liquidate immediately
their outstanding cash advances and the concerned officials to adhere
strictly to the rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances provided under COA Circular 97-002;
c) enforce the appropriate sanctions provided by rules and regulations upon
failure by the concerned AOs despite their receipt of the demand letter
issued by the Municipal Accountant; and
d) ensure that outstanding cash advances are liquidated prior to separation
from office by any appointed or elected officer or employee.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
a) Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit a copy of its Assessment Roll to
the Municipal Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of
December each year;
b) Municipal Treasurer to submit a duly certified list of the name of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance
with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs; and
c) Municipal Accountant to gather complete source documents before
drawing a Journal Entry Voucher for setting up the Real Property Tax
and Special Education Tax Receivables as enjoined under Section 20 of
NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that Management:
a) review the existing practices on inventory management and implement the
Perpetual Inventory Method System in accounting for the receipts and
issuances of supplies and materials required under the NGAS Manual for
LGUs;
b) enforce to the heads of offices/departments the immediate submission of
relevant supporting documents upon distribution or consumption of the
inventories under their custody for the timely preparation of SSMI by the
Municipal Treasurer; and
c) instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of
supplies and materials by type and prepare the Report of the Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI), subject to reconciliation between records of
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) create an inventory
committee composed of regular employees whose only duties or functions shall be to
conduct on a full time basis, an actual physical count of PPEs for a specified period of
time, to facilitate proper accounting and reporting of fixed properties; and (b)
designate one personnel each from MTO and Accounting Office who shall conduct
the reconciliation of respective records to present the accurate value of PPE in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the following measures to
ensure correctness of the Road Networks account:
a) create an Inventory Committee, preferably composed of one personnel each
from the municipal Planning and Development Office; Engineering Office;
Treasurer’s Office; General Services Office; Accountant’s Office and Assessor
Office who shall conduct, on a full-time basis and within specified time,
inspection and inventory of all road networks;
b) require the created inventory committee to conduct a physical
count/inventory of all the municipal roads and reconcile it with the Municipal
Road Map, if any and determine the existence and condition of the road
components of each road for proper measurement and valuation using the
prescribed forms such as: a) Local Road and Bridge Inventory and Condition
Survey; b) Report of Local Road Network; and c) Report on the Physical
Count of Local Road Network;
c) direct the Municipal Accounting Office to: gather all details and information
needed to determine the cost of the road lot, road components and
depreciable life of each road network and reconcile it with the recorded cost in
the Registry of Public Infrastructure and existing ledgers; prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the recognition of Public Infrastructure
- Roads, Highways and Bridges in the General Ledger amounting to
₱30,117,502.22 as of December 31, 2019 ensuring that the accounting and
reporting procedures laid down under COA Circular 2015-008 are fully
complied by the concerned offices while the same procedure shall be
undertaken for other public infrastructures accounts;
d) instruct the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office to
strictly prepare and maintain all the prescribed reports under COA Circular
2015-008;
e) require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to maintain the
information on the local road network of the MGB, particularly, the plans and
programs of the construction, repair and maintenance of local roads and the
depreciable life of the road networks in coordination with the Engineering
Office;
f) direct the Municipal Engineering Office to ensure that the Local Road
Network map is aligned to the requirement of a road map described under the
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2017-159 and Local Roads Assets
Management Manual for LGUs; and, as the Office that undertakes the
construction and repair of the local roads, documents relative thereto shall be
maintained so that the necessary and detailed information of a certain road
project, such as complete description of the road and the cost segregation of
the road components are available anytime needed by the accounting and
general services office and other stakeholders;
g) require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to prepare and maintain the tax maps,
which is used as basis for identifying the ownership of all the road lots in the
municipal road network and shall also assist in determining the cost of the
road lots with no available costs; and
h) instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to gather and keep in her vault
documents regarding the ownership or acquisition of road land or road right
of way through purchase, donation or any other modes of acquisition.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accounting Office to:
a) gather the details/information records, and other alternative documents
needed to determine the components of local roads identified in the inventory;
b) segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with
identified cost and recognize component without corresponding cost at its fair
value;
c) depreciate the carrying amount of existing roads and its components to reflect
the asset in its used condition and shall serve as the basis for recording in the
general ledger;
d) determine the life of the local roads within the range prescribed by COA
taking into consideration the following factors: a. design b. users c. volume of
users;
e) compute separately the depreciation for the road components taking into
consideration the initial cost and useful life.
f) ensure that the details and information must be indicated in the Property,
Plant and Equipment Ledger Card - Local Road Network Ledger Card
prepared and maintained for each item of road network;
g) re-compute the accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense from CY
2016 to CY 2019 and thereafter adjust the affected accounts and the net book
value of each of the asset; and
h) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the year-end balances of the
Public Infrastructure and Government Equity accounts.

We recommended that Management:
a) continue to trace and retrieve relevant documents to serve as basis in
analysing prior and current year reconciling items and prepare necessary
adjustment, if any;
b) conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the
difference and effect any adjustment if any.
c) strictly adhere to COA Circular 2015-009 or the Revised Chart of Accounts
for Local Government Units and pertinent provisions of P.D. 1445 on the
proper account identification, classification and recording.

We recommended that Management revisit DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular
No. 2017-1 on the allowable expenditures chargeable against the SEF and only defray
funds to the actual needs of their beneficiaries, in order to optimally utilize the scarce
government resources.

We recommended that Management:
a) direct the GFPS-TWG to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report on a

b) enjoin the MPDO to spearhead the preparation and completion of the

c) devise an applicable scheme wherein the list of disbursements charged against
the GAD Fund may be provided by the Municipal Accounting Office to the

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that all
disclosure requirement in the Notes to the Financial Statements be included to

performance of the Municipal Government of Bombon.

direct the Municipal Assessor to exert more effort in the titling of all parcels of land declared under the name of the Municipality of Bombon and furnish the Municipal Accountant with such documents to support the ownership of the lots as recorded in the books;

require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete set of accounting records to support the balance of Land account in the financial statements such as property ledger cards indicating all the information necessary in ascertaining the valuation and ownership of the assets; and

instruct the General Services Officer to determine the acquisition cost or fair value, as the case may be, of the lots which may have been recorded in the books, but with undetermined individual value, and to acquire custody of all the titles and deeds of real and personal properties of the Municipality of Bombon.
observations.reason: Management asserted that they already filed for registration of the lots owned by the Municipality before the Register of Deeds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the cash in bank balances in order to establish the accuracy of the Cash in Bank – LCCA and LCSA accounts.
observations.reason: There is an on-going reconciliation of records. The Municipality lacks personnel who will focus solely on the reconciliation of records. Transfers to National Government Agencies and promotion of various accounting personnel have also contributed to the delay in the reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strategize on the optimum use of the Municipal Accounting Office’s workforce in order to analyze the accounts and reconcile the GL and SL balances.
observations.reason: There is an on-going reconciliation of records. The Municipality lacks personnel who will focus solely on the reconciliation of records. Transfers to National Government Agencies and promotion of various accounting personnel have also contributed to the delay in the reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

reconstitute the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council that it may adhere to the structure prescribed by law and have it well represented by various sectors for comprehensive preparation, mitigation, response and rehabilitation during disasters;

create the mandated positions for LDRRMO to assist the Officer-Designate and perform their assigned duties in administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning; and

instruct the MDRRM Officer-Designate to prepare annually the LDRRMFIP in the proper format to be attached upon release of LDRRM Fund as supporting document in the validity of fund utilization; and prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund, for submission to the Auditor.

We recommended that Management direct all concerned officials involved in the planning and budgeting of the 20% Local Development Fund to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office and deliberate the necessity and feasibility of projects for implementation in the ensuing year to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant reconciles the book balances of the Cash in Bank with the bank balances as of December 31, 2014 to properly account and report the correct cash in bank balances; and 

Items in the bank reconciliation statements that are long outstanding should be given immediate attention and should be adjusted in the books duly supported with complete supporting documentations for verification purposes.
observations.reason: There is an on-going reconciliation of records. The Municipality lacks personnel who will focus solely on the reconciliation of records. Transfers to National Government Agencies and promotion of various accounting personnel have also contributed to the delay in the reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the bookkeeper to reconcile the balance of the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in all funds by effecting the necessary accounting entry/adjustments to bring into agreement the balances of the reciprocal accounts. Likewise, require the concerned officials/employees to be extra cautious in the recording of the transactions.
observations.reason: There is an on-going reconciliation of records. The Municipality lacks personnel who will focus solely on the reconciliation of records. Transfers to National Government Agencies and promotion of various accounting personnel have also contributed to the delay in the reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management maintain subsidiary ledger for all inventory and other accounts for an accurate recording and presentation in the financial statement.

We recommended that management require the: 

Supply Officer to submit the SSMI supported with RIS/Utilization Reports for all issued supplies procured in the previous and current years to the Municipal Accountant so that proper accounting thereof could be made; and

Municipal Accountant to properly account the procured supplies under appropriate Inventory accounts. All issuances made therefrom should be supported with SSMI, RIS/Utilization Reports and debited against the appropriate expense accounts.
observations.reason: There is an on-going reconciliation. The Municipality lacks personel who will focus solely on the maintenance of inventories/ledgers/cards and lacks warehouse/room for the purchased supplies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:

Submit the required documentation for the above paid transactions to establish the validity and propriety of the claims; and

Ensure that before payments are made, all the required documents are attached to the DVs and liquidations to prevent suspension of transactions which may mature into disallowances if not settled within ninety (90) days from receipt of Notices of Suspension.
observations.reason: Management has an on-going reconciliation and completion of documents. The person responsible for the retrieval of various documents from the Accounting files have already transferred to another agency. Thus, the Municipality lacks personnel who will focus solely on the reconciliation of recommendations with the accounting files.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer/General Service Officer to imprint in the government motor vehicle the required markings pursuant to COA Circular No. 75-6 and extra effort be exerted to follow-up from the Transportation Office (LTO) the issuance of the required government plate (Red Plate).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should implement the procedures on Check Disbursement in compliance with Section 44 of NGAS, Volume I for LGUs wherein the concerned office shall help to facilitate the fast processing of check payment for the purchased goods and services of the Municipality.  This will encourage more suppliers/creditors to participate in bidding or give price quotations to the advantage of the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should give each office copy of the Check Disbursement Process and blank DVs and other required forms to facilitate fast processing of check payment.
observations.reason: The changes in the personnel who are handling the documents of various suppliers/contractors greatly affected the disbursement process as well as the increased volume of transactions w/o the much needed additional manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer should immediately notify this Office of the result of the SP review of the Municipality’s appropriation ordinance for CY 2013 or their inaction after 90 days upon receipt of the ordinance to guide the Audit Team with the actions to be taken concerning the CY2013 disbursements.

We further recommended that in the succeeding years, the Local Chief Executive should submit the budget of the ensuing year to the Sangguniang Bayan not later than the sixteenth (16th) of October of the current fiscal year for their approval on or before the end of the current fiscal year.  In this manner, early submission and timely review of the appropriation ordinance authorizing the annual budget by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan can be facilitated.

We recommended that all municipal officials and employees who were paid with Yuletide Assistance be required to refund immediately the amount received by them unless a valid legal basis be presented to the Audit Team to support the disbursements.
observations.reason: There is an on-going refund of the Yuletide assistance by various officials and employees.
